test_series_name: Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994 - 12 Main Tests
notes: These tests are based on the uploaded PDF on the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994.
The sequence follows a progression from foundational recall to broader application and mixed-topic reasoning.
Time limits increase toward later tests.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid consecutive repetition of the same answer letter.

tests.title: Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994 - Main Test 1
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the four main Article 1 conditions for accepting transaction value?

options.A: Existence of a bill of lading, packing list, certificate of origin, and customs declaration
options.B: Use of CIF terms, insurance, invoice, and appeal rights
options.C: No disqualifying restrictions, no undeterminable conditions, no unadjusted proceeds accruing to the seller, and no unacceptable related-party influence on price
options.D: Identical goods, similar goods, deductive value, and computed value

questions.explanation: These are the core Article 1.1 conditions.

questions.question: What is the safest summary formula for transaction value?

options.A: Transaction value equals domestic price plus freight
options.B: Transaction value equals cost of production plus profit in every case
options.C: Transaction value equals the highest available market price
options.D: Transaction value equals price actually paid or payable plus only those Article 8 additions that lawfully apply

questions.explanation: This is the safest summary formula.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? If the importer requests it, how must customs communicate its grounds in a related-party case?

options.A: In writing
options.B: Only to the carrier
options.C: Only through the seller
options.D: Verbally only

questions.explanation: The grounds must be provided in writing on request.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is one way the importer can show related-party transaction value is acceptable under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: By showing the importer prefers Article 1
options.B: By showing the value closely approximates the transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods for export to the same country of importation
options.C: By showing the goods were sold CIF
options.D: By showing the price is the highest available in the market

questions.explanation: Importers may use test values for comparison.

questions.question: What does the note to Article 1 say about the price actually paid or payable?

options.A: It is the total payment made or to be made by the buyer to or for the benefit of the seller, whether direct or indirect, and not necessarily only a money transfer
options.B: It excludes all indirect payments
options.C: It includes only bank wire transfers
options.D: It means only the invoice subtotal

questions.explanation: The note broadens understanding of the actual price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 4 allow the importer to do?

options.A: Replace Article 1 with Article 2
options.B: Request reversal of the order of Articles 5 and 6
options.C: Request immediate use of Article 7
options.D: Ignore Article 8 additions

questions.explanation: Article 4 permits reversal of deductive and computed value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 1.1(c) deal with?

options.A: Only inland warehousing charges
options.B: Only post-clearance penalties
options.C: Only transport costs after importation
options.D: Proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use of the goods that accrue directly or indirectly to the seller

questions.explanation: Article 1.1(c) addresses resale proceeds flowing back to the seller.

questions.question: What does Article 15 define as 'customs value of imported goods'?

options.A: The production cost of the goods
options.B: The insured value of the goods only
options.C: The value of goods for the purpose of levying ad valorem duties of customs on imported goods
options.D: The domestic resale value of the goods

questions.explanation: Article 15 contains important definitions.

questions.question: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.A: They automatically convert the method to Article 5
options.B: They may be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished in line with the note to Article 1
options.C: They require use of minimum values
options.D: They must always be added as Article 8 costs

questions.explanation: Certain post-import charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Must Members provide a final right of appeal to a judicial authority?

options.A: Yes
options.B: No
options.C: Only for Article 7 cases
options.D: Only for developing countries

questions.explanation: A final appeal to a judicial authority is required.

questions.question: What does 'without penalty' mean in the note to Article 11?

options.A: Customs must refund duty automatically
options.B: All legal costs must be waived
options.C: The importer cannot be required to pay assessed duty before appealing
options.D: The importer is not fined merely for exercising the right of appeal, though normal court costs and lawyers’ fees may still apply

questions.explanation: Without penalty does not eliminate normal court costs.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? If more than one transaction value of similar goods is found under Article 3, which one is used?

options.A: The one with the most expensive freight
options.B: The latest one chronologically
options.C: The lowest such value
options.D: The highest such value

questions.explanation: The lowest qualifying value is selected.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What types of restrictions do not prevent use of transaction value under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Only restrictions imposed by carriers
options.B: Restrictions imposed by law or public authorities, restrictions limiting geographical area of resale, and restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods
options.C: Only restrictions imposed after importation
options.D: Any restriction imposed by the seller

questions.explanation: These three categories do not automatically bar Method 1.

questions.question: Can transaction value still be used if proceeds accrue to the seller?

options.A: Only if the importer is unrelated to the seller
options.B: No, never
options.C: Only if the goods are identical goods
options.D: Yes, if an appropriate adjustment can be made in accordance with Article 8

questions.explanation: Relevant proceeds can be handled through Article 8 adjustments.

questions.question: The buyer supplied free molds used to manufacture the imported goods. They are not already included in the invoice. What is the correct customs valuation concept?

options.A: An assist under Article 8.1(b) that may need to be added
options.B: A buying commission
options.C: A post-import deduction
options.D: A prohibited minimum value

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 1?

options.A: Identical goods under Article 2
options.B: Deductive value under Article 5
options.C: Transaction value under Article 1
options.D: Fallback method under Article 7

questions.explanation: Method 1 is transaction value.

questions.question: An officer proposes using the domestic selling price of locally produced goods because it is easy and gives a higher value. What is the correct response?

options.A: That basis is prohibited under Article 7
options.B: It is allowed if customs explains the reason
options.C: It is required where Article 1 fails
options.D: It is allowed only for related-party sales

questions.explanation: Domestic selling price of locally produced goods is prohibited.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Article 8 mainly about?

options.A: It regulates customs penalties
options.B: It describes fallback valuation only
options.C: It contains the importer’s right of appeal
options.D: It sets out the additions that may be made to the price actually paid or payable when determining transaction value under Article 1

questions.explanation: Article 8 covers additions to transaction value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can a Member require a non-resident producer to produce accounts for computed value purposes?

options.A: Only where transport was by sea
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: No
options.D: Only when the importer is related to the producer

questions.explanation: A Member cannot compel a non-resident producer to produce accounts.

questions.question: What does Article 8.1(a)(i) include?

options.A: Only post-import transport
options.B: Commissions and brokerage, except buying commissions
options.C: Only inland taxes
options.D: Only buying commissions

questions.explanation: Selling commissions and brokerage can be additions; buying commissions are excluded.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are arbitrary or fictitious values allowed under Article 7?

options.A: Only where invoices are missing
options.B: Yes, if identical goods are unavailable
options.C: No
options.D: Yes, if customs suspects undervaluation

questions.explanation: Arbitrary and fictitious values are prohibited.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What must customs do if it believes the relationship influenced the price?

options.A: It must communicate its grounds to the importer and give the importer a reasonable opportunity to respond
options.B: It must reject the value without explanation
options.C: It must move directly to Article 7
options.D: It must immediately impose a minimum value

questions.explanation: The importer must get notice and a chance to respond.

questions.question: What do the interpretative notes say about the order of valuation methods?

options.A: They allow customs to begin with Article 7
options.B: They require random method selection
options.C: They eliminate Article 4
options.D: They confirm that Articles 1 through 7 must be applied sequentially, except that the importer may request reversal of Articles 5 and 6

questions.explanation: The notes reinforce the method sequence.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 2?

options.A: Transaction value of similar goods under Article 3
options.B: Transaction value of identical goods under Article 2
options.C: Fallback method under Article 7
options.D: Computed value under Article 6

questions.explanation: Method 2 is identical goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 1.1(b) prohibit?

options.A: All related-party transactions
options.B: All sales involving royalties
options.C: Use of transaction value where the sale or price is subject to a condition or consideration whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued
options.D: All discounts given by the seller

questions.explanation: Undeterminable conditions can defeat Method 1.

questions.question: What are the three test values under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: Only Article 2, Article 3, and Article 7 values
options.B: Transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods, customs value under Article 5, and customs value under Article 6
options.C: Minimum values, reference prices, and fallback values
options.D: Domestic market prices, production costs, and insurance values

questions.explanation: These are the comparison points under Article 1.2(b).

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What stockholding threshold is mentioned in Article 15.4(d)?

options.A: Five percent or more of the outstanding voting stock or shares of both buyer and seller held by one person
options.B: Fifty percent
options.C: Ten percent
options.D: One percent

questions.explanation: This is the threshold mentioned.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Does the mere fact that buyer and seller are related automatically make transaction value unacceptable?

options.A: Yes
options.B: Only where transport costs are missing
options.C: Only in sea freight
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Relationship alone is not enough.

questions.question: What exchange rate should be used under Article 9?

options.A: The rate duly published by the competent authorities of the country of importation, reflecting as effectively as possible the current value of the currency in commercial transactions
options.B: The rate used by the exporter in its invoice
options.C: Only the central bank buying rate
options.D: Any exchange rate chosen by the importer

questions.explanation: Article 9 requires a duly published relevant rate.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are employer and employee related persons under Article 15.4?

options.A: No
options.B: Only if they also hold voting stock
options.C: Only in developing countries
options.D: Yes

questions.explanation: Employer-employee is one related-person category.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does the note to Article 5 explain with 'greatest aggregate quantity'?

options.A: It means the highest price for one unit
options.B: It means the total quantity imported in a year
options.C: The unit price used is the one at which the greatest number of units is sold to unrelated buyers at the first commercial level after importation
options.D: It means the quantity sold only to related buyers

questions.explanation: This note clarifies Article 5.

questions.question: What kind of criteria should customs valuation use according to the Agreement?

options.A: Only production costs in the exporting country
options.B: Simple and equitable criteria consistent with commercial practices
options.C: The highest value available in the market
options.D: Arbitrary benchmark prices

questions.explanation: The Agreement prefers equitable criteria consistent with commercial practice.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Name another category of assists.

options.A: Only the importer’s own staff salaries
options.B: Only post-import maintenance charges
options.C: Tools, dies, moulds, and similar items used in the production of the imported goods
options.D: Only customs declaration charges

questions.explanation: Production tooling can be an assist.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What types of restrictions do not prevent use of transaction value under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Any restriction imposed by the seller
options.B: Only restrictions imposed by carriers
options.C: Only restrictions imposed after importation
options.D: Restrictions imposed by law or public authorities, restrictions limiting geographical area of resale, and restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods

questions.explanation: These three categories do not automatically bar Method 1.

questions.question: What does the note to Article 5 explain with 'greatest aggregate quantity'?

options.A: The unit price used is the one at which the greatest number of units is sold to unrelated buyers at the first commercial level after importation
options.B: It means the total quantity imported in a year
options.C: It means the highest price for one unit
options.D: It means the quantity sold only to related buyers

questions.explanation: This note clarifies Article 5.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A Member faces a recurring technical problem on how to apply the valuation rules to a new business model and wants interpretative guidance. Which body is most relevant?

options.A: Only the producer’s domestic tax authority
options.B: The Technical Committee on Customs Valuation
options.C: Only the importing country’s carrier
options.D: Only the Council for Trade in Goods

questions.explanation: The Technical Committee handles technical interpretation issues.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? When may an Article 8 addition be made?

options.A: Whenever customs wants to raise revenue
options.B: Only for sea freight imports
options.C: Only if the importer is related to the seller
options.D: When the relevant cost is incurred by the buyer, is not already included in the price, and is based on objective and quantifiable data

questions.explanation: These are the general requirements for lawful additions.

questions.question: Can transaction value still be used if proceeds accrue to the seller?

options.A: No, never
options.B: Yes, if an appropriate adjustment can be made in accordance with Article 8
options.C: Only if the goods are identical goods
options.D: Only if the importer is unrelated to the seller

questions.explanation: Relevant proceeds can be handled through Article 8 adjustments.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can transaction value still be used if proceeds accrue to the seller?

options.A: Only if the importer is unrelated to the seller
options.B: No, never
options.C: Yes, if an appropriate adjustment can be made in accordance with Article 8
options.D: Only if the goods are identical goods

questions.explanation: Relevant proceeds can be handled through Article 8 adjustments.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A Member faces a recurring technical problem on how to apply the valuation rules to a new business model and wants interpretative guidance. Which body is most relevant?

options.A: The Technical Committee on Customs Valuation
options.B: Only the producer’s domestic tax authority
options.C: Only the importing country’s carrier
options.D: Only the Council for Trade in Goods

questions.explanation: The Technical Committee handles technical interpretation issues.

questions.question: What does the note to Article 5 explain with 'greatest aggregate quantity'?

options.A: It means the highest price for one unit
options.B: It means the quantity sold only to related buyers
options.C: It means the total quantity imported in a year
options.D: The unit price used is the one at which the greatest number of units is sold to unrelated buyers at the first commercial level after importation

questions.explanation: This note clarifies Article 5.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What right does Article 11 give the importer?

options.A: A right of appeal, without penalty, against a customs valuation determination
options.B: A right to refuse all customs inquiries
options.C: A right to avoid all duties pending appeal forever
options.D: A right to choose any valuation method

questions.explanation: The importer has a right of appeal.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the basis of value under Article 2?

options.A: The producer’s cost of identical goods
options.B: The domestic price of like goods in the importing country
options.C: The transaction value of identical goods sold for export to the same country of importation and exported at or about the same time as the goods being valued
options.D: The higher of available identical-goods prices

questions.explanation: Article 2 uses transaction value of qualifying identical goods.

questions.question: What does Article 8.1(d) require customs to add?

options.A: All profits made by the importer after resale
options.B: The value of any part of the proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use of the imported goods that accrues directly or indirectly to the seller
options.C: All freight refunds
options.D: All domestic taxes paid by the importer

questions.explanation: Proceeds flowing back to the seller may need to be added.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does the note to Article 8 say about objective and quantifiable data?

options.A: Data is optional if the importer is related
options.B: The importer may substitute domestic prices
options.C: If such data do not exist for required additions, transaction value cannot be determined under Article 1
options.D: Customs may freely estimate additions without data

questions.explanation: Objective and quantifiable data is essential.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does the note to Article 1 say about the price actually paid or payable?

options.A: It means only the invoice subtotal
options.B: It includes only bank wire transfers
options.C: It excludes all indirect payments
options.D: It is the total payment made or to be made by the buyer to or for the benefit of the seller, whether direct or indirect, and not necessarily only a money transfer

questions.explanation: The note broadens understanding of the actual price.

questions.question: What does the note to Article 1 say about activities undertaken by the buyer on the buyer’s own account?

options.A: They replace Article 8 additions
options.B: They are not treated as indirect payments to the seller unless Article 8 specifically requires adjustment
options.C: They always bar use of transaction value
options.D: They are always added to customs value

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities are not automatically dutiable.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? How should assists be apportioned according to the note to Article 8?

options.A: In a reasonable manner appropriate to the circumstances and in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles
options.B: Equally over all future imports regardless of evidence
options.C: Only to the first shipment
options.D: According to the highest possible estimate

questions.explanation: Reasonable documented apportionment is required.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the three test values under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: Only Article 2, Article 3, and Article 7 values
options.B: Transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods, customs value under Article 5, and customs value under Article 6
options.C: Minimum values, reference prices, and fallback values
options.D: Domestic market prices, production costs, and insurance values

questions.explanation: These are the comparison points under Article 1.2(b).

questions.question: What do the interpretative notes say about the order of valuation methods?

options.A: They allow customs to begin with Article 7
options.B: They require random method selection
options.C: They confirm that Articles 1 through 7 must be applied sequentially, except that the importer may request reversal of Articles 5 and 6
options.D: They eliminate Article 4

questions.explanation: The notes reinforce the method sequence.

tests.title: Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994 - Main Test 2
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer presents an invoice showing a real sale for export, but the contract says the buyer must also purchase large quantities of unrelated goods, and the value of that condition cannot be determined for the imported goods. What is the main Article 1 problem?

options.A: A currency conversion issue under Article 9
options.B: A publication problem under Article 12
options.C: A condition or consideration under Article 1.1(b) whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued
options.D: A lawful geographical resale restriction

questions.explanation: An undeterminable condition can defeat transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the fallback method under Article 7?

options.A: The highest price found in any export market
options.B: A mandatory minimum customs value
options.C: A value based on any domestic benchmark chosen by customs
options.D: A customs value determined using reasonable means consistent with the principles and general provisions of the Agreement and Article VII of GATT 1994, based on data available in the country of importation

questions.explanation: Fallback remains constrained by WTO principles.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Imported goods are sold after importation in the local market to unrelated buyers in different quantities and at different prices. Customs needs a deductive value. What concept is most important?

options.A: The unit price at the greatest aggregate quantity
options.B: The producer’s fabrication cost
options.C: The importer’s preferred price
options.D: The highest resale price found anywhere

questions.explanation: Greatest aggregate quantity is central to Article 5.

questions.question: What types of restrictions do not prevent use of transaction value under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Only restrictions imposed after importation
options.B: Restrictions imposed by law or public authorities, restrictions limiting geographical area of resale, and restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods
options.C: Any restriction imposed by the seller
options.D: Only restrictions imposed by carriers

questions.explanation: These three categories do not automatically bar Method 1.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? When applying Article 2, what kind of adjustments may be made?

options.A: Adjustments based on domestic retail profit only
options.B: Arbitrary adjustments chosen by customs
options.C: Adjustments for any seller preference
options.D: Adjustments for differences in commercial level, quantity, and significant differences in transport and related Article 8(2) costs

questions.explanation: Reasonable adjustments may be made when justified by evidence.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 2?

options.A: Transaction value of identical goods under Article 2
options.B: Fallback method under Article 7
options.C: Transaction value of similar goods under Article 3
options.D: Computed value under Article 6

questions.explanation: Method 2 is identical goods.

questions.question: What does the note to Article 6 say about computed value in practice?

options.A: Computed value is based only on domestic selling price
options.B: Computed value must always be used before Article 5
options.C: Computed value is generally limited to cases where the producer is prepared to supply the needed cost information and verification can be arranged
options.D: Computed value never uses producer information

questions.explanation: The note explains why Article 6 is often difficult.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 4?

options.A: Fallback method under Article 7
options.B: Deductive value under Article 5
options.C: Computed value under Article 6
options.D: Identical goods under Article 2

questions.explanation: Method 4 is deductive value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs add amounts not listed in Article 8?

options.A: Yes, if customs explains the reason in writing
options.B: Yes, if they are commercially significant
options.C: No
options.D: Yes, if domestic law allows broader additions

questions.explanation: No additions may be made except as provided in Article 8.

questions.question: When may an Article 8 addition be made?

options.A: Whenever customs wants to raise revenue
options.B: Only if the importer is related to the seller
options.C: Only for sea freight imports
options.D: When the relevant cost is incurred by the buyer, is not already included in the price, and is based on objective and quantifiable data

questions.explanation: These are the general requirements for lawful additions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is one responsibility of the Technical Committee under Annex II?

options.A: To examine specific technical problems in daily administration of the valuation system and give advisory opinions on solutions
options.B: To publish all commercial invoices
options.C: To hear judicial appeals
options.D: To set national exchange rates

questions.explanation: The Technical Committee deals with technical interpretation problems.

questions.question: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.A: They automatically convert the method to Article 5
options.B: They may be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished in line with the note to Article 1
options.C: They require use of minimum values
options.D: They must always be added as Article 8 costs

questions.explanation: Certain post-import charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: Are minimum customs values allowed under Article 7?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes, if published in advance
options.C: Only for developing countries permanently
options.D: Only for related-party transactions

questions.explanation: Minimum customs values are prohibited.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the basis of value under Article 3?

options.A: The domestic price of similar goods in the country of importation
options.B: The transaction value of similar goods sold for export to the same country of importation and exported at or about the same time as the goods being valued
options.C: The customs value of any unrelated goods
options.D: The price of goods exported to a third country

questions.explanation: Article 3 uses qualifying similar goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does 'greatest aggregate quantity' mean?

options.A: The total value of all imports in a month
options.B: The highest price charged for a single unit
options.C: The lowest quantity sold at retail
options.D: The price at which the greatest number of units is sold at the first commercial level after importation to unrelated buyers

questions.explanation: This is a key concept in deductive value.

questions.question: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

options.A: Article 13 on release of goods
options.B: Article 22 on national legislation
options.C: Article 11 gives a right of appeal without penalty
options.D: Article 9 on currency conversion

questions.explanation: Article 11 protects the right of appeal.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer presents an invoice showing a real sale for export, but the contract says the buyer must also purchase large quantities of unrelated goods, and the value of that condition cannot be determined for the imported goods. What is the main Article 1 problem?

options.A: A lawful geographical resale restriction
options.B: A currency conversion issue under Article 9
options.C: A publication problem under Article 12
options.D: A condition or consideration under Article 1.1(b) whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued

questions.explanation: An undeterminable condition can defeat transaction value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What exchange rate should be used under Article 9?

options.A: The rate used by the exporter in its invoice
options.B: Only the central bank buying rate
options.C: The rate duly published by the competent authorities of the country of importation, reflecting as effectively as possible the current value of the currency in commercial transactions
options.D: Any exchange rate chosen by the importer

questions.explanation: Article 9 requires a duly published relevant rate.

questions.question: Are employer and employee related persons under Article 15.4?

options.A: Only if they also hold voting stock
options.B: Yes
options.C: Only in developing countries
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Employer-employee is one related-person category.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 8.3 require for all additions?

options.A: They must be based on objective and quantifiable data
options.B: They may be based on the highest market value
options.C: They must be based on customs estimates
options.D: They may be based on domestic reference prices

questions.explanation: Objective and quantifiable data is mandatory.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 8.2 allow each Member to decide in its legislation?

options.A: Whether to replace transaction value with minimum values
options.B: Whether to ignore Article 8 completely
options.C: Whether to use only deductive value
options.D: Whether to include or exclude in whole or in part transport costs, loading/unloading and handling charges, and insurance costs to the port or place of importation

questions.explanation: Members choose their Article 8.2 treatment in national law.

questions.question: What kind of criteria should customs valuation use according to the Agreement?

options.A: Simple and equitable criteria consistent with commercial practices
options.B: Only production costs in the exporting country
options.C: The highest value available in the market
options.D: Arbitrary benchmark prices

questions.explanation: The Agreement prefers equitable criteria consistent with commercial practice.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is one way the importer can show related-party transaction value is acceptable under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: By showing the price is the highest available in the market
options.B: By showing the value closely approximates the transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods for export to the same country of importation
options.C: By showing the importer prefers Article 1
options.D: By showing the goods were sold CIF

questions.explanation: Importers may use test values for comparison.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are charges for the right to reproduce imported goods in the country of importation added to customs value?

options.A: Only if the goods are copyrighted
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: No
options.D: Only if transport is by air

questions.explanation: Reproduction rights in the importing country are excluded.

questions.question: Name one category of related persons under Article 15.4.

options.A: They insure their goods with the same insurer
options.B: They are officers or directors of one another’s businesses
options.C: They use the same customs broker
options.D: They import through the same port

questions.explanation: This is one of the listed categories.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are charges for the right to reproduce imported goods in the country of importation added to customs value?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: Only if transport is by air
options.D: Only if the goods are copyrighted

questions.explanation: Reproduction rights in the importing country are excluded.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Imported goods are sold after importation in the local market to unrelated buyers in different quantities and at different prices. Customs needs a deductive value. What concept is most important?

options.A: The producer’s fabrication cost
options.B: The highest resale price found anywhere
options.C: The importer’s preferred price
options.D: The unit price at the greatest aggregate quantity

questions.explanation: Greatest aggregate quantity is central to Article 5.

questions.question: An importer presents an invoice showing a real sale for export, but the contract says the buyer must also purchase large quantities of unrelated goods, and the value of that condition cannot be determined for the imported goods. What is the main Article 1 problem?

options.A: A publication problem under Article 12
options.B: A lawful geographical resale restriction
options.C: A condition or consideration under Article 1.1(b) whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued
options.D: A currency conversion issue under Article 9

questions.explanation: An undeterminable condition can defeat transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Name another valuation basis prohibited under Article 7.

options.A: Objective and quantifiable Article 8 data
options.B: A system that provides for acceptance of the higher of two alternative values
options.C: Test values used by the importer for comparison
options.D: The lowest of qualifying identical-goods values

questions.explanation: Higher-of-two-values systems are prohibited.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can further processed goods be valued under Article 5?

options.A: Only if the seller is unrelated
options.B: Only if customs applies Article 7 first
options.C: Yes, if the importer requests it, with allowance for value added by processing and the required deductions
options.D: No, never

questions.explanation: Further processed goods may be used in certain circumstances.

questions.question: What exchange rate should be used under Article 9?

options.A: The rate duly published by the competent authorities of the country of importation, reflecting as effectively as possible the current value of the currency in commercial transactions
options.B: Any exchange rate chosen by the importer
options.C: Only the central bank buying rate
options.D: The rate used by the exporter in its invoice

questions.explanation: Article 9 requires a duly published relevant rate.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 10 require?

options.A: That customs never use documents in court
options.B: That all customs documents be made public immediately
options.C: That confidentiality applies only to related-party sales
options.D: That confidential information provided for customs valuation purposes be treated as strictly confidential and not disclosed without specific permission, except where disclosure is required in judicial proceedings

questions.explanation: Article 10 protects confidential information.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the fallback method under Article 7?

options.A: A customs value determined using reasonable means consistent with the principles and general provisions of the Agreement and Article VII of GATT 1994, based on data available in the country of importation
options.B: The highest price found in any export market
options.C: A value based on any domestic benchmark chosen by customs
options.D: A mandatory minimum customs value

questions.explanation: Fallback remains constrained by WTO principles.

questions.question: If more than one transaction value of similar goods is found under Article 3, which one is used?

options.A: The one with the most expensive freight
options.B: The lowest such value
options.C: The highest such value
options.D: The latest one chronologically

questions.explanation: The lowest qualifying value is selected.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs wants to use computed value, but the foreign producer refuses to provide cost accounts and has no presence in the importing country. What practical limitation is illustrated?

options.A: Article 6 automatically becomes Article 7
options.B: Article 6 can only be used for identical goods
options.C: Article 6 requires domestic market prices only
options.D: Computed value is difficult when the producer is unwilling to provide the necessary information and cannot be compelled as a non-resident

questions.explanation: The Agreement limits compulsion of non-resident producers.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the three test values under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: Minimum values, reference prices, and fallback values
options.B: Domestic market prices, production costs, and insurance values
options.C: Transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods, customs value under Article 5, and customs value under Article 6
options.D: Only Article 2, Article 3, and Article 7 values

questions.explanation: These are the comparison points under Article 1.2(b).

questions.question: Scenario variant: The buyer supplied free molds used to manufacture the imported goods. They are not already included in the invoice. What is the correct customs valuation concept?

options.A: An assist under Article 8.1(b) that may need to be added
options.B: A post-import deduction
options.C: A prohibited minimum value
options.D: A buying commission

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is deductive value under Article 5 based on?

options.A: The producer’s materials and fabrication costs
options.B: The unit price at which the imported goods, or identical or similar imported goods, are sold in the country of importation in the greatest aggregate quantity to unrelated buyers
options.C: A minimum reference value fixed by customs
options.D: The domestic price of goods produced in the importing country

questions.explanation: Article 5 starts from domestic resale price and deducts specified items.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A customs officer discovers buyer and seller are related and immediately rejects transaction value without explanation. Which core rule has been ignored?

options.A: Article 7 minimum values
options.B: Article 12 publication requirements
options.C: Article 8.2 on freight and insurance
options.D: Relationship alone is not enough; customs must examine whether the relationship influenced the price and communicate concerns to the importer

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone does not automatically invalidate Method 1.

questions.question: What does Article 15 define as 'customs value of imported goods'?

options.A: The production cost of the goods
options.B: The insured value of the goods only
options.C: The value of goods for the purpose of levying ad valorem duties of customs on imported goods
options.D: The domestic resale value of the goods

questions.explanation: Article 15 contains important definitions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 3?

options.A: Transaction value of similar goods under Article 3
options.B: Transaction value under Article 1
options.C: Deductive value under Article 5
options.D: Computed value under Article 6

questions.explanation: Method 3 is similar goods.

questions.question: The importer pays a royalty for a trademark connected to the imported goods, and payment is required by the seller as a condition of sale. What is the likely treatment?

options.A: It is only relevant after domestic resale
options.B: The royalty may need to be added if it relates to the goods, is a condition of sale, and is not already included in the price
options.C: It must always be excluded
options.D: It becomes an Article 5 deduction

questions.explanation: This fits the core royalty test.

questions.question: Can transaction value still be used if proceeds accrue to the seller?

options.A: No, never
options.B: Only if the goods are identical goods
options.C: Only if the importer is unrelated to the seller
options.D: Yes, if an appropriate adjustment can be made in accordance with Article 8

questions.explanation: Relevant proceeds can be handled through Article 8 adjustments.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Name another valuation basis prohibited under Article 7.

options.A: Test values used by the importer for comparison
options.B: The lowest of qualifying identical-goods values
options.C: A system that provides for acceptance of the higher of two alternative values
options.D: Objective and quantifiable Article 8 data

questions.explanation: Higher-of-two-values systems are prohibited.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 3?

options.A: Transaction value of similar goods under Article 3
options.B: Transaction value under Article 1
options.C: Computed value under Article 6
options.D: Deductive value under Article 5

questions.explanation: Method 3 is similar goods.

questions.question: What does Article 22 require of Members?

options.A: Each Member may replace Article 1 with domestic market prices
options.B: Each Member must publish all importer records
options.C: Each Member may keep inconsistent laws indefinitely
options.D: Each Member must ensure that its laws, regulations, and administrative procedures conform to the Agreement and must inform the Committee of relevant changes

questions.explanation: Article 22 links national law to the Agreement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What institutions are established under Article 18?

options.A: The SPS and TBT Committees
options.B: The Committee on Customs Valuation at the WTO and the Technical Committee on Customs Valuation under the WCO/CCC
options.C: Only a single national committee in each Member
options.D: Only the Dispute Settlement Body

questions.explanation: Article 18 establishes these institutions.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Article 8 mainly about?

options.A: It describes fallback valuation only
options.B: It regulates customs penalties
options.C: It sets out the additions that may be made to the price actually paid or payable when determining transaction value under Article 1
options.D: It contains the importer’s right of appeal

questions.explanation: Article 8 covers additions to transaction value.

questions.question: What is one way the importer can show related-party transaction value is acceptable under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: By showing the value closely approximates the transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods for export to the same country of importation
options.B: By showing the goods were sold CIF
options.C: By showing the price is the highest available in the market
options.D: By showing the importer prefers Article 1

questions.explanation: Importers may use test values for comparison.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 12 require?

options.A: That all import invoices be published
options.B: That only tariff schedules be published
options.C: That customs valuation decisions be kept secret
options.D: That laws, regulations, judicial decisions, and administrative rulings of general application giving effect to the Agreement be published

questions.explanation: Article 12 requires publication of general rules.

tests.title: Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994 - Main Test 3
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What are assists under Article 8.1(b)?

options.A: Any post-import marketing expense
options.B: Any service performed by customs
options.C: Goods and services supplied directly or indirectly by the buyer free of charge or at reduced cost for use in connection with production and sale for export of the imported goods
options.D: Any domestic tax borne by the importer

questions.explanation: Assists are buyer-provided inputs or services tied to the imported goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does the note to Article 8 say about objective and quantifiable data?

options.A: If such data do not exist for required additions, transaction value cannot be determined under Article 1
options.B: The importer may substitute domestic prices
options.C: Data is optional if the importer is related
options.D: Customs may freely estimate additions without data

questions.explanation: Objective and quantifiable data is essential.

questions.question: What is computed value under Article 6 based on?

options.A: A customs-set benchmark price
options.B: The domestic retail selling price after importation
options.C: The higher of two alternative customs values
options.D: Cost or value of materials and fabrication, an amount for profit and general expenses, and the costs needed to reflect the Member’s Article 8(2) option

questions.explanation: Article 6 builds value from production cost elements.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is one way the importer can show related-party transaction value is acceptable under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: By showing the importer prefers Article 1
options.B: By showing the value closely approximates the transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods for export to the same country of importation
options.C: By showing the price is the highest available in the market
options.D: By showing the goods were sold CIF

questions.explanation: Importers may use test values for comparison.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? If more than one transaction value of identical goods is found under Article 2, which one is used?

options.A: The lowest such value
options.B: The average of all values
options.C: The highest such value
options.D: The one nearest to the importer’s declared value

questions.explanation: The lowest qualifying value is used.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer presents an invoice showing a real sale for export, but the contract says the buyer must also purchase large quantities of unrelated goods, and the value of that condition cannot be determined for the imported goods. What is the main Article 1 problem?

options.A: A publication problem under Article 12
options.B: A lawful geographical resale restriction
options.C: A currency conversion issue under Article 9
options.D: A condition or consideration under Article 1.1(b) whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued

questions.explanation: An undeterminable condition can defeat transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can transaction value still be used if proceeds accrue to the seller?

options.A: No, never
options.B: Yes, if an appropriate adjustment can be made in accordance with Article 8
options.C: Only if the importer is unrelated to the seller
options.D: Only if the goods are identical goods

questions.explanation: Relevant proceeds can be handled through Article 8 adjustments.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? If the importer requests it, how must customs communicate its grounds in a related-party case?

options.A: Verbally only
options.B: Only to the carrier
options.C: In writing
options.D: Only through the seller

questions.explanation: The grounds must be provided in writing on request.

questions.question: An importer presents an invoice showing a real sale for export, but the contract says the buyer must also purchase large quantities of unrelated goods, and the value of that condition cannot be determined for the imported goods. What is the main Article 1 problem?

options.A: A publication problem under Article 12
options.B: A condition or consideration under Article 1.1(b) whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued
options.C: A currency conversion issue under Article 9
options.D: A lawful geographical resale restriction

questions.explanation: An undeterminable condition can defeat transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? If more than one transaction value of identical goods is found under Article 2, which one is used?

options.A: The one nearest to the importer’s declared value
options.B: The highest such value
options.C: The lowest such value
options.D: The average of all values

questions.explanation: The lowest qualifying value is used.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What must customs do if it believes the relationship influenced the price?

options.A: It must communicate its grounds to the importer and give the importer a reasonable opportunity to respond
options.B: It must reject the value without explanation
options.C: It must move directly to Article 7
options.D: It must immediately impose a minimum value

questions.explanation: The importer must get notice and a chance to respond.

questions.question: Are minimum customs values allowed under Article 7?

options.A: Only for related-party transactions
options.B: Only for developing countries permanently
options.C: Yes, if published in advance
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Minimum customs values are prohibited.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 8.1(d) require customs to add?

options.A: All freight refunds
options.B: The value of any part of the proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use of the imported goods that accrues directly or indirectly to the seller
options.C: All profits made by the importer after resale
options.D: All domestic taxes paid by the importer

questions.explanation: Proceeds flowing back to the seller may need to be added.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are charges for the right to reproduce imported goods in the country of importation added to customs value?

options.A: Only if the goods are copyrighted
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: No
options.D: Only if transport is by air

questions.explanation: Reproduction rights in the importing country are excluded.

questions.question: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

options.A: Article 11 gives a right of appeal without penalty
options.B: Article 22 on national legislation
options.C: Article 13 on release of goods
options.D: Article 9 on currency conversion

questions.explanation: Article 11 protects the right of appeal.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 4?

options.A: Fallback method under Article 7
options.B: Computed value under Article 6
options.C: Identical goods under Article 2
options.D: Deductive value under Article 5

questions.explanation: Method 4 is deductive value.

questions.question: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.A: They may be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished in line with the note to Article 1
options.B: They require use of minimum values
options.C: They automatically convert the method to Article 5
options.D: They must always be added as Article 8 costs

questions.explanation: Certain post-import charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: Are arbitrary or fictitious values allowed under Article 7?

options.A: Yes, if customs suspects undervaluation
options.B: Yes, if identical goods are unavailable
options.C: Only where invoices are missing
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Arbitrary and fictitious values are prohibited.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Name another category of assists.

options.A: Only post-import maintenance charges
options.B: Only customs declaration charges
options.C: Tools, dies, moulds, and similar items used in the production of the imported goods
options.D: Only the importer’s own staff salaries

questions.explanation: Production tooling can be an assist.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 8.1(a)(i) include?

options.A: Only inland taxes
options.B: Commissions and brokerage, except buying commissions
options.C: Only buying commissions
options.D: Only post-import transport

questions.explanation: Selling commissions and brokerage can be additions; buying commissions are excluded.

questions.question: What types of restrictions do not prevent use of transaction value under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Any restriction imposed by the seller
options.B: Only restrictions imposed by carriers
options.C: Only restrictions imposed after importation
options.D: Restrictions imposed by law or public authorities, restrictions limiting geographical area of resale, and restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods

questions.explanation: These three categories do not automatically bar Method 1.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Name one valuation basis prohibited under Article 7.

options.A: Previously determined customs values used with flexibility
options.B: The selling price in the country of importation of goods produced in that country
options.C: Reasonable application of earlier methods
options.D: Data available in the country of importation

questions.explanation: Domestic selling price of locally produced goods is prohibited.

questions.question: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.A: They may be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished in line with the note to Article 1
options.B: They automatically convert the method to Article 5
options.C: They require use of minimum values
options.D: They must always be added as Article 8 costs

questions.explanation: Certain post-import charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: To maximize customs revenue regardless of trade effects
options.B: To require use of domestic market prices
options.C: To provide a fair, uniform, and neutral system for valuing goods for customs purposes and prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values
options.D: To make minimum values compulsory

questions.explanation: This is the central objective of the Agreement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does the note to Article 7 say about the fallback method?

options.A: Fallback requires minimum customs values
options.B: Fallback valuation should, as far as possible, be based on previously determined customs values and use the methods of Articles 1 to 6 with reasonable flexibility
options.C: Fallback must always use domestic market prices
options.D: Fallback may use any arbitrary value

questions.explanation: The note limits fallback through reasonable flexibility.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Transaction value cannot be used. Customs finds two possible identical-goods transaction values that both satisfy Article 2. Which should be used?

options.A: The lower of the two qualifying values
options.B: Whichever value customs prefers
options.C: Their average
options.D: The higher of the two qualifying values

questions.explanation: Under Article 2 the lowest qualifying value is used.

questions.question: What charges are excluded from customs value if distinguished from the price actually paid or payable under the note to Article 1?

options.A: All royalties and licence fees
options.B: All commissions and brokerage
options.C: All freight and insurance before importation
options.D: Post-importation construction, erection, assembly, maintenance, or technical assistance, post-import transport, and duties and taxes of the country of importation

questions.explanation: Certain post-import and national-tax charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the basis of value under Article 2?

options.A: The producer’s cost of identical goods
options.B: The higher of available identical-goods prices
options.C: The transaction value of identical goods sold for export to the same country of importation and exported at or about the same time as the goods being valued
options.D: The domestic price of like goods in the importing country

questions.explanation: Article 2 uses transaction value of qualifying identical goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the safest summary formula for transaction value?

options.A: Transaction value equals domestic price plus freight
options.B: Transaction value equals price actually paid or payable plus only those Article 8 additions that lawfully apply
options.C: Transaction value equals cost of production plus profit in every case
options.D: Transaction value equals the highest available market price

questions.explanation: This is the safest summary formula.

questions.question: What does the note to Article 6 say about computed value in practice?

options.A: Computed value never uses producer information
options.B: Computed value is based only on domestic selling price
options.C: Computed value must always be used before Article 5
options.D: Computed value is generally limited to cases where the producer is prepared to supply the needed cost information and verification can be arranged

questions.explanation: The note explains why Article 6 is often difficult.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What deductions are made under Article 5?

options.A: Only customs duty and VAT, with no other deductions
options.B: Only post-import storage
options.C: Commissions or usual profit and general expenses, inland transport and insurance, Article 8(2) costs where appropriate, and customs duties and other national taxes
options.D: Only insurance and freight

questions.explanation: These are the main deductions under Article 5.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does 'without penalty' mean in the note to Article 11?

options.A: The importer is not fined merely for exercising the right of appeal, though normal court costs and lawyers’ fees may still apply
options.B: Customs must refund duty automatically
options.C: All legal costs must be waived
options.D: The importer cannot be required to pay assessed duty before appealing

questions.explanation: Without penalty does not eliminate normal court costs.

questions.question: Can further processed goods be valued under Article 5?

options.A: Only if customs applies Article 7 first
options.B: Yes, if the importer requests it, with allowance for value added by processing and the required deductions
options.C: No, never
options.D: Only if the seller is unrelated

questions.explanation: Further processed goods may be used in certain circumstances.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are charges for the right to reproduce imported goods in the country of importation added to customs value?

options.A: Only if transport is by air
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: Only if the goods are copyrighted
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Reproduction rights in the importing country are excluded.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 8.1(d) require customs to add?

options.A: All profits made by the importer after resale
options.B: All freight refunds
options.C: The value of any part of the proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use of the imported goods that accrues directly or indirectly to the seller
options.D: All domestic taxes paid by the importer

questions.explanation: Proceeds flowing back to the seller may need to be added.

questions.question: What is the fallback method under Article 7?

options.A: A customs value determined using reasonable means consistent with the principles and general provisions of the Agreement and Article VII of GATT 1994, based on data available in the country of importation
options.B: A value based on any domestic benchmark chosen by customs
options.C: A mandatory minimum customs value
options.D: The highest price found in any export market

questions.explanation: Fallback remains constrained by WTO principles.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? If more than one transaction value of identical goods is found under Article 2, which one is used?

options.A: The highest such value
options.B: The lowest such value
options.C: The one nearest to the importer’s declared value
options.D: The average of all values

questions.explanation: The lowest qualifying value is used.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are dividends or other payments that do not relate to the imported goods part of customs value according to the note to Article 1?

options.A: Yes, always
options.B: Only if paid after resale
options.C: No
options.D: Only in related-party transactions

questions.explanation: Unrelated dividends are not part of customs value.

questions.question: Imported goods are sold after importation in the local market to unrelated buyers in different quantities and at different prices. Customs needs a deductive value. What concept is most important?

options.A: The unit price at the greatest aggregate quantity
options.B: The importer’s preferred price
options.C: The highest resale price found anywhere
options.D: The producer’s fabrication cost

questions.explanation: Greatest aggregate quantity is central to Article 5.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can further processed goods be valued under Article 5?

options.A: No, never
options.B: Only if the seller is unrelated
options.C: Only if customs applies Article 7 first
options.D: Yes, if the importer requests it, with allowance for value added by processing and the required deductions

questions.explanation: Further processed goods may be used in certain circumstances.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 10 require?

options.A: That confidential information provided for customs valuation purposes be treated as strictly confidential and not disclosed without specific permission, except where disclosure is required in judicial proceedings
options.B: That confidentiality applies only to related-party sales
options.C: That all customs documents be made public immediately
options.D: That customs never use documents in court

questions.explanation: Article 10 protects confidential information.

questions.question: What must customs examine when buyer and seller are related?

options.A: Only the tariff classification
options.B: The circumstances surrounding the sale to determine whether the relationship influenced the price
options.C: Only the route of transport
options.D: Only the insurance policy

questions.explanation: The key issue is influence on price.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the basis of value under Article 3?

options.A: The price of goods exported to a third country
options.B: The domestic price of similar goods in the country of importation
options.C: The transaction value of similar goods sold for export to the same country of importation and exported at or about the same time as the goods being valued
options.D: The customs value of any unrelated goods

questions.explanation: Article 3 uses qualifying similar goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 6?

options.A: Domestic price method
options.B: Highest-of-two-values method
options.C: Minimum value method
options.D: Fallback method under Article 7

questions.explanation: Method 6 is fallback.

questions.question: A customs officer discovers buyer and seller are related and immediately rejects transaction value without explanation. Which core rule has been ignored?

options.A: Relationship alone is not enough; customs must examine whether the relationship influenced the price and communicate concerns to the importer
options.B: Article 12 publication requirements
options.C: Article 7 minimum values
options.D: Article 8.2 on freight and insurance

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone does not automatically invalidate Method 1.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the primary basis for customs value under the Agreement?

options.A: Deductive value under Article 5
options.B: Transaction value as defined in Article 1
options.C: Computed value under Article 6
options.D: Minimum customs values

questions.explanation: The Agreement gives primacy to transaction value under Article 1.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is Method 4?

options.A: Fallback method under Article 7
options.B: Identical goods under Article 2
options.C: Deductive value under Article 5
options.D: Computed value under Article 6

questions.explanation: Method 4 is deductive value.

questions.question: What right does Article 16 give the importer?

options.A: The right to waive Article 8 additions
options.B: The right to stop all customs inquiries
options.C: The right to choose the exchange rate
options.D: Upon written request, the importer has the right to a written explanation of how the customs value was determined

questions.explanation: Importers can request a written explanation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What charges are excluded from customs value if distinguished from the price actually paid or payable under the note to Article 1?

options.A: All freight and insurance before importation
options.B: Post-importation construction, erection, assembly, maintenance, or technical assistance, post-import transport, and duties and taxes of the country of importation
options.C: All commissions and brokerage
options.D: All royalties and licence fees

questions.explanation: Certain post-import and national-tax charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 20 allow developing country Members to do?

options.A: Use arbitrary values permanently
options.B: Ignore Article 8 indefinitely
options.C: Avoid all notification obligations
options.D: Delay application of the Agreement for a limited time and receive technical assistance

questions.explanation: Article 20 provides limited special treatment and assistance.
