questions.explanation: The Agreement states that valuation procedures should not be used to combat dumping.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What right does Article 16 give the importer?

options.A: The right to stop all customs inquiries
options.B: The right to choose the exchange rate
options.C: The right to waive Article 8 additions
options.D: Upon written request, the importer has the right to a written explanation of how the customs value was determined

questions.explanation: Importers can request a written explanation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the most important customs valuation principle to remember for exams?

options.A: Transaction value is the primary basis, but it only applies if Article 1 conditions are met and any required Article 8 adjustments are made
options.B: Minimum values are the safest customs method
options.C: Fallback value should be used first whenever customs has doubts
options.D: Related-party sales are always rejected

questions.explanation: This is the core exam principle.

questions.question: The importer pays a royalty for a trademark connected to the imported goods, and payment is required by the seller as a condition of sale. What is the likely treatment?

options.A: It becomes an Article 5 deduction
options.B: The royalty may need to be added if it relates to the goods, is a condition of sale, and is not already included in the price
options.C: It must always be excluded
options.D: It is only relevant after domestic resale

questions.explanation: This fits the core royalty test.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 8.1(a)(i) include?

options.A: Only buying commissions
options.B: Only post-import transport
options.C: Commissions and brokerage, except buying commissions
options.D: Only inland taxes

questions.explanation: Selling commissions and brokerage can be additions; buying commissions are excluded.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What protection does Article 13 give the importer when valuation is delayed?

options.A: The importer automatically pays no duty
options.B: The importer may withdraw the goods from customs if sufficient guarantee is provided to cover the possible final duty liability
options.C: The goods must remain in customs until final litigation ends
options.D: The importer may ignore all customs controls

questions.explanation: Release against guarantee protects the importer.

questions.question: What right does Article 16 give the importer?

options.A: The right to stop all customs inquiries
options.B: The right to choose the exchange rate
options.C: The right to waive Article 8 additions
options.D: Upon written request, the importer has the right to a written explanation of how the customs value was determined

questions.explanation: Importers can request a written explanation.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Name another category of assists.

options.A: Only the importer’s own staff salaries
options.B: Only post-import maintenance charges
options.C: Tools, dies, moulds, and similar items used in the production of the imported goods
options.D: Only customs declaration charges

questions.explanation: Production tooling can be an assist.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Should valuation procedures be used to combat dumping?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: Only for identical goods
options.D: Only where prices are low

questions.explanation: The Agreement states that valuation procedures should not be used to combat dumping.

questions.question: Are payments for the right to distribute or resell imported goods always added to customs value?

options.A: No, never
options.B: Only if paid through a bank
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: No; they are not added if they are not a condition of the sale for export to the country of importation

questions.explanation: Distribution/resale rights need condition-of-sale analysis.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can a Member require a non-resident producer to produce accounts for computed value purposes?

options.A: No
options.B: Only where transport was by sea
options.C: Only when the importer is related to the producer
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: A Member cannot compel a non-resident producer to produce accounts.

questions.question: A customs officer discovers buyer and seller are related and immediately rejects transaction value without explanation. Which core rule has been ignored?

options.A: Article 7 minimum values
options.B: Relationship alone is not enough; customs must examine whether the relationship influenced the price and communicate concerns to the importer
options.C: Article 12 publication requirements
options.D: Article 8.2 on freight and insurance

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone does not automatically invalidate Method 1.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement?

options.A: To require use of domestic market prices
options.B: To maximize customs revenue regardless of trade effects
options.C: To provide a fair, uniform, and neutral system for valuing goods for customs purposes and prevent arbitrary or fictitious customs values
options.D: To make minimum values compulsory

questions.explanation: This is the central objective of the Agreement.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? How should assists be apportioned according to the note to Article 8?

options.A: Only to the first shipment
options.B: According to the highest possible estimate
options.C: Equally over all future imports regardless of evidence
options.D: In a reasonable manner appropriate to the circumstances and in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles

questions.explanation: Reasonable documented apportionment is required.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.A: They may be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished in line with the note to Article 1
options.B: They must always be added as Article 8 costs
options.C: They require use of minimum values
options.D: They automatically convert the method to Article 5

questions.explanation: Certain post-import charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: What is the rule in Article 1.1(d) about related parties?

options.A: Related-party sales can only be valued under Article 5
options.B: All related-party sales must be rejected
options.C: If buyer and seller are related, transaction value may still be used if it is acceptable for customs purposes under Article 1.2
options.D: Related-party sales always move directly to fallback method

questions.explanation: Relatedness does not automatically defeat transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is one way the importer can show related-party transaction value is acceptable under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: By showing the price is the highest available in the market
options.B: By showing the value closely approximates the transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods for export to the same country of importation
options.C: By showing the importer prefers Article 1
options.D: By showing the goods were sold CIF

questions.explanation: Importers may use test values for comparison.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can a Member require a non-resident producer to produce accounts for computed value purposes?

options.A: Only when the importer is related to the producer
options.B: Yes, always
options.C: No
options.D: Only where transport was by sea

questions.explanation: A Member cannot compel a non-resident producer to produce accounts.

questions.question: What kind of criteria should customs valuation use according to the Agreement?

options.A: Arbitrary benchmark prices
options.B: Simple and equitable criteria consistent with commercial practices
options.C: The highest value available in the market
options.D: Only production costs in the exporting country

questions.explanation: The Agreement prefers equitable criteria consistent with commercial practice.

questions.question: The buyer supplied free molds used to manufacture the imported goods. They are not already included in the invoice. What is the correct customs valuation concept?

options.A: A buying commission
options.B: A prohibited minimum value
options.C: A post-import deduction
options.D: An assist under Article 8.1(b) that may need to be added

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does the note to Article 8 say a buying commission means?

options.A: A fee paid by an importer to the importer’s agent for representing the importer abroad in the purchase of the goods being valued
options.B: A domestic resale commission paid after importation
options.C: A fee paid to the seller’s agent to secure the sale
options.D: A customs broker’s duty calculation fee

questions.explanation: This is the note’s definition of a buying commission.

questions.question: What does Article 8.1(a)(iii) include?

options.A: Only labour, never materials
options.B: Only materials, never labour
options.C: Only post-import repacking costs
options.D: The cost of packing, whether for labour or materials

questions.explanation: Packing costs include both labour and materials.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 1.1(b) prohibit?

options.A: All discounts given by the seller
options.B: Use of transaction value where the sale or price is subject to a condition or consideration whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued
options.C: All sales involving royalties
options.D: All related-party transactions

questions.explanation: Undeterminable conditions can defeat Method 1.

tests.title: Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994 - Main Test 6
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.

Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What right does Article 16 give the importer?

options.A: The right to waive Article 8 additions
options.B: The right to choose the exchange rate
options.C: Upon written request, the importer has the right to a written explanation of how the customs value was determined
options.D: The right to stop all customs inquiries

questions.explanation: Importers can request a written explanation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does the note to Article 8 say about objective and quantifiable data?

options.A: Data is optional if the importer is related
options.B: The importer may substitute domestic prices
options.C: Customs may freely estimate additions without data
options.D: If such data do not exist for required additions, transaction value cannot be determined under Article 1

questions.explanation: Objective and quantifiable data is essential.

questions.question: Are charges for the right to reproduce imported goods in the country of importation added to customs value?

options.A: Only if transport is by air
options.B: No
options.C: Only if the goods are copyrighted
options.D: Yes, always

questions.explanation: Reproduction rights in the importing country are excluded.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 2?

options.A: Transaction value of identical goods under Article 2
options.B: Fallback method under Article 7
options.C: Computed value under Article 6
options.D: Transaction value of similar goods under Article 3

questions.explanation: Method 2 is identical goods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is computed value under Article 6 based on?

options.A: A customs-set benchmark price
options.B: Cost or value of materials and fabrication, an amount for profit and general expenses, and the costs needed to reflect the Member’s Article 8(2) option
options.C: The domestic retail selling price after importation
options.D: The higher of two alternative customs values

questions.explanation: Article 6 builds value from production cost elements.

questions.question: What kind of criteria should customs valuation use according to the Agreement?

options.A: Only production costs in the exporting country
options.B: Arbitrary benchmark prices
options.C: Simple and equitable criteria consistent with commercial practices
options.D: The highest value available in the market

questions.explanation: The Agreement prefers equitable criteria consistent with commercial practice.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? If the importer requests it, how must customs communicate its grounds in a related-party case?

options.A: In writing
options.B: Verbally only
options.C: Only to the carrier
options.D: Only through the seller

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What do the notes say about generally accepted accounting principles?

options.A: Only exporters’ internal estimates matter
options.B: GAAP is irrelevant in valuation
options.C: Only the importing country’s GAAP matters for every article
options.D: Customs administrations should use information prepared in a manner consistent with generally accepted accounting principles appropriate to the country relevant for the article in question

questions.explanation: The notes reference appropriate GAAP.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

options.A: Article 13 on release of goods
options.B: Article 9 on currency conversion
options.C: Article 11 gives a right of appeal without penalty
options.D: Article 22 on national legislation

questions.explanation: Article 11 protects the right of appeal.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What exchange rate should be used under Article 9?

options.A: Only the central bank buying rate
options.B: The rate used by the exporter in its invoice
options.C: Any exchange rate chosen by the importer
options.D: The rate duly published by the competent authorities of the country of importation, reflecting as effectively as possible the current value of the currency in commercial transactions

questions.explanation: Article 9 requires a duly published relevant rate.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name another valuation basis prohibited under Article 7.

options.A: A system that provides for acceptance of the higher of two alternative values
options.B: Objective and quantifiable Article 8 data
options.C: Test values used by the importer for comparison
options.D: The lowest of qualifying identical-goods values

questions.explanation: Higher-of-two-values systems are prohibited.

questions.question: Why should customs value be based, as far as possible, on transaction value?

options.A: Because it avoids use of invoices
options.B: Because transaction value reflects the actual commercial transaction for the goods being valued
options.C: Because it always gives the highest customs value
options.D: Because it is based on domestic resale prices

questions.explanation: Transaction value is tied to the real commercial sale.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 4 allow the importer to do?

options.A: Request reversal of the order of Articles 5 and 6
options.B: Replace Article 1 with Article 2
options.C: Ignore Article 8 additions
options.D: Request immediate use of Article 7

questions.explanation: Article 4 permits reversal of deductive and computed value.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are the three test values under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: Only Article 2, Article 3, and Article 7 values
options.B: Domestic market prices, production costs, and insurance values
options.C: Minimum values, reference prices, and fallback values
options.D: Transaction value in sales to unrelated buyers of identical or similar goods, customs value under Article 5, and customs value under Article 6

questions.question: What types of restrictions do not prevent use of transaction value under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Any restriction imposed by the seller
options.B: Only restrictions imposed by carriers
options.C: Restrictions imposed by law or public authorities, restrictions limiting geographical area of resale, and restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods
options.D: Only restrictions imposed after importation

questions.explanation: These three categories do not automatically bar Method 1.

questions.question: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.A: They must always be added as Article 8 costs
options.B: They may be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished in line with the note to Article 1
options.C: They require use of minimum values
options.D: They automatically convert the method to Article 5

questions.explanation: Certain post-import charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.A: They automatically convert the method to Article 5
options.B: They require use of minimum values
options.C: They may be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished in line with the note to Article 1
options.D: They must always be added as Article 8 costs

questions.explanation: Certain post-import charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: Why must Article 1 be read together with Article 8?

options.A: Because Article 8 provides the adjustments that may need to be added to the price actually paid or payable when determining transaction value
options.B: Because Article 8 deals only with customs penalties
options.C: Because Article 8 replaces Article 1 entirely
options.D: Because Article 8 only applies to fallback valuation

questions.explanation: Article 8 contains the lawful additions to transaction value.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An officer proposes using the domestic selling price of locally produced goods because it is easy and gives a higher value. What is the correct response?

options.A: It is required where Article 1 fails
options.B: It is allowed only for related-party sales
options.C: It is allowed if customs explains the reason
options.D: That basis is prohibited under Article 7

questions.explanation: Domestic selling price of locally produced goods is prohibited.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Must the valuation methods be applied in sequence?

options.A: Only the importer chooses the method
options.B: Yes, except that Articles 5 and 6 may be reversed at the importer’s request
options.C: No, customs may choose freely
options.D: Only Articles 1 to 3 must be sequential

questions.explanation: The methods follow a sequence, with the Article 4 reversal option.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer presents an invoice showing a real sale for export, but the contract says the buyer must also purchase large quantities of unrelated goods, and the value of that condition cannot be determined for the imported goods. What is the main Article 1 problem?

options.A: A condition or consideration under Article 1.1(b) whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued
options.B: A currency conversion issue under Article 9
options.C: A publication problem under Article 12
options.D: A lawful geographical resale restriction

questions.explanation: An undeterminable condition can defeat transaction value.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why must Article 1 be read together with Article 8?

options.A: Because Article 8 only applies to fallback valuation
options.B: Because Article 8 provides the adjustments that may need to be added to the price actually paid or payable when determining transaction value
options.C: Because Article 8 deals only with customs penalties
options.D: Because Article 8 replaces Article 1 entirely

questions.explanation: Article 8 contains the lawful additions to transaction value.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A Member faces a recurring technical problem on how to apply the valuation rules to a new business model and wants interpretative guidance. Which body is most relevant?

options.A: Only the producer’s domestic tax authority
options.B: Only the Council for Trade in Goods

options.D: Only the importing country’s carrier

options.C: Only Article 2, Article 3, and Article 7 values

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What protection does Article 13 give the importer when valuation is delayed?

options.A: The importer may ignore all customs controls
options.B: The goods must remain in customs until final litigation ends
options.C: The importer may withdraw the goods from customs if sufficient guarantee is provided to cover the possible final duty liability

options.A: Any exchange rate chosen by the importer
options.B: The rate duly published by the competent authorities of the country of importation, reflecting as effectively as possible the current value of the currency in commercial transactions
options.C: Only the central bank buying rate

options.A: Article 6 requires domestic market prices only
options.B: Article 6 can only be used for identical goods

questions.explanation: The Agreement limits compulsion of non-resident producers.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does the note to Article 1 say about activities undertaken by the buyer on the buyer’s own account?

options.A: They replace Article 8 additions
options.B: They are not treated as indirect payments to the seller unless Article 8 specifically requires adjustment
options.C: They always bar use of transaction value
options.D: They are always added to customs value

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities are not automatically dutiable.

questions.question: What is the basis of value under Article 3?

options.A: The transaction value of similar goods sold for export to the same country of importation and exported at or about the same time as the goods being valued
options.B: The domestic price of similar goods in the country of importation

options.D: The price of goods exported to a third country

options.A: Jump directly to Article 7
options.B: Apply the higher of two possible values
options.C: Move sequentially to Articles 2, 3, 5, 6, and finally 7, with possible reversal of 5 and 6 at the importer’s request

options.B: Automatic waiver of all duties

options.D: Release of goods against a sufficient guarantee under Article 13

options.A: Currency conversion for customs valuation purposes

options.C: Release of goods against guarantee
options.D: Importer appeal rights

options.A: Only where transport was by sea

questions.explanation: A Member cannot compel a non-resident producer to produce accounts.

options.A: An assist under Article 8.1(b) that may need to be added
options.B: A post-import deduction
options.C: A prohibited minimum value
options.D: A buying commission

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 23 require?

options.A: The Technical Committee must hear appeals annually

options.C: Each Member must recalculate all customs values yearly
options.D: The Committee must review annually the implementation and operation of the Agreement and report developments to the Council for Trade in Goods

questions.question: What does Article 4 allow the importer to do?

options.A: Replace Article 1 with Article 2

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does 'without penalty' mean in the note to Article 11?

options.A: The importer cannot be required to pay assessed duty before appealing
options.B: The importer is not fined merely for exercising the right of appeal, though normal court costs and lawyers’ fees may still apply
options.C: All legal costs must be waived
options.D: Customs must refund duty automatically

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are dividends or other payments that do not relate to the imported goods part of customs value according to the note to Article 1?

options.A: No
options.B: Only if paid after resale
options.C: Only in related-party transactions

questions.question: What does Article 8.2 allow each Member to decide in its legislation?

options.A: Whether to replace transaction value with minimum values
options.B: Whether to include or exclude in whole or in part transport costs, loading/unloading and handling charges, and insurance costs to the port or place of importation

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What is the fourth category of assists under Article 8.1(b)(iv)?

questions.question: Are dividends or other payments that do not relate to the imported goods part of customs value according to the note to Article 1?

options.A: Only in related-party transactions

options.B: Only post-import maintenance charges
options.C: Tools, dies, moulds, and similar items used in the production of the imported goods

options.A: If such data do not exist for required additions, transaction value cannot be determined under Article 1
options.B: Customs may freely estimate additions without data
options.C: The importer may substitute domestic prices
options.D: Data is optional if the importer is related

questions.explanation: Objective and quantifiable data is essential.

questions.question: What reservation may developing countries seek regarding Article 4?

options.A: They may reserve the right to use domestic prices always
options.B: They may reserve the right to avoid all Article 8 additions
options.C: They may reserve the right to skip directly to Article 7
options.D: They may reserve the right to allow reversal of Articles 5 and 6 only when customs authorities agree to the request

options.A: Only where transport was by sea

questions.question: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

questions.question: Scenario variant: Imported goods are sold after importation in the local market to unrelated buyers in different quantities and at different prices. Customs needs a deductive value. What concept is most important?

options.A: The highest resale price found anywhere

questions.question: Are minimum customs values allowed under Article 7?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Are dividends or other payments that do not relate to the imported goods part of customs value according to the note to Article 1?

questions.question: What does 'greatest aggregate quantity' mean?

questions.question: Scenario variant: An officer proposes using the domestic selling price of locally produced goods because it is easy and gives a higher value. What is the correct response?

options.C: It is allowed only for related-party sales

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Does the mere fact that buyer and seller are related automatically make transaction value unacceptable?

options.A: Only where transport costs are missing

questions.question: What does Article 23 require?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the basis of value under Article 3?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 12 require?

options.B: Release of goods against a sufficient guarantee under Article 13

options.D: Mandatory use of fallback valuation

questions.explanation: Article 13 protects the importer when valuation is delayed.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.B: They automatically convert the method to Article 5

options.A: The importer may ignore all customs controls

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? If more than one transaction value of identical goods is found under Article 2, which one is used?

options.A: The highest such value
options.B: The lowest such value
options.C: The average of all values
options.D: The one nearest to the importer’s declared value

questions.question: What is Method 5?

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can customs add amounts not listed in Article 8?

options.A: Yes, if they are commercially significant

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs valuation is delayed, but the importer urgently needs the goods for production. What safeguard may be available?

questions.question: What is the fourth category of assists under Article 8.1(b)(iv)?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What institutions are established under Article 18?

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does the note to Article 8 say about objective and quantifiable data?

questions.question: Scenario variant: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 4 allow the importer to do?

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are arbitrary or fictitious values allowed under Article 7?

questions.question: When applying Article 2, what kind of adjustments may be made?

questions.question: Scenario variant: The buyer supplied free molds used to manufacture the imported goods. They are not already included in the invoice. What is the correct customs valuation concept?

options.A: A buying commission

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are payments for the right to distribute or resell imported goods always added to customs value?

questions.question: Are charges for the right to reproduce imported goods in the country of importation added to customs value?

questions.question: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Name one category of related persons under Article 15.4.

questions.question: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

options.A: Article 22 on national legislation

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 3?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What are 'identical goods' under Article 15?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Why should customs value be based, as far as possible, on transaction value?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What kind of criteria should customs valuation use according to the Agreement?

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are dividends or other payments that do not relate to the imported goods part of customs value according to the note to Article 1?

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is Method 6?

options.A: Highest-of-two-values method
options.B: Domestic price method
options.C: Minimum value method
options.D: Fallback method under Article 7

questions.explanation: Method 6 is fallback.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 8.1(a)(ii) include?

options.A: Any container used anywhere in the supply chain
options.B: The cost of containers treated as being one for customs purposes with the goods
options.C: Domestic retail display packaging only after import
options.D: The importer’s warehouse rent

questions.explanation: Certain container costs may be added.

questions.question: What deductions are made under Article 5?

options.A: Commissions or usual profit and general expenses, inland transport and insurance, Article 8(2) costs where appropriate, and customs duties and other national taxes
options.B: Only post-import storage
options.C: Only insurance and freight
options.D: Only customs duty and VAT, with no other deductions

questions.explanation: These are the main deductions under Article 5.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What must customs examine when buyer and seller are related?

options.A: Only the insurance policy
options.B: Only the tariff classification
options.C: Only the route of transport
options.D: The circumstances surrounding the sale to determine whether the relationship influenced the price

questions.explanation: The key issue is influence on price.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 15 define as 'customs value of imported goods'?

options.A: The insured value of the goods only
options.B: The domestic resale value of the goods
options.C: The value of goods for the purpose of levying ad valorem duties of customs on imported goods
options.D: The production cost of the goods

questions.explanation: Article 15 contains important definitions.

questions.question: What does the note to Article 5 explain with 'greatest aggregate quantity'?

options.A: It means the quantity sold only to related buyers
options.B: It means the highest price for one unit
options.C: It means the total quantity imported in a year
options.D: The unit price used is the one at which the greatest number of units is sold to unrelated buyers at the first commercial level after importation

questions.explanation: This note clarifies Article 5.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An officer proposes using the domestic selling price of locally produced goods because it is easy and gives a higher value. What is the correct response?

options.A: It is required where Article 1 fails
options.B: That basis is prohibited under Article 7
options.C: It is allowed only for related-party sales
options.D: It is allowed if customs explains the reason

questions.explanation: Domestic selling price of locally produced goods is prohibited.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 9 deal with?

options.A: Currency conversion for customs valuation purposes
options.B: Release of goods against guarantee
options.C: Importer appeal rights
options.D: Related-person definitions

questions.explanation: Article 9 governs currency conversion.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A customs officer discovers buyer and seller are related and immediately rejects transaction value without explanation. Which core rule has been ignored?

options.A: Article 8.2 on freight and insurance
options.B: Article 7 minimum values
options.C: Relationship alone is not enough; customs must examine whether the relationship influenced the price and communicate concerns to the importer
options.D: Article 12 publication requirements

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone does not automatically invalidate Method 1.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 9 deal with?

options.A: Currency conversion for customs valuation purposes
options.B: Related-person definitions
options.C: Importer appeal rights
options.D: Release of goods against guarantee

questions.explanation: Article 9 governs currency conversion.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Should valuation procedures be used to combat dumping?

options.A: Only for identical goods
options.B: No
options.C: Yes, always
options.D: Only where prices are low

questions.explanation: The Agreement states that valuation procedures should not be used to combat dumping.

questions.question: Transaction value cannot be used. Customs finds two possible identical-goods transaction values that both satisfy Article 2. Which should be used?

options.A: Whichever value customs prefers
options.B: The higher of the two qualifying values
options.C: Their average
options.D: The lower of the two qualifying values

questions.explanation: Under Article 2 the lowest qualifying value is used.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Can the Technical Committee facilitate technical assistance?

options.A: No
options.B: Only for developed countries
options.C: Yes
options.D: Only in Article 7 cases

questions.explanation: Technical assistance is one of its roles.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? How should assists be apportioned according to the note to Article 8?

options.A: Only to the first shipment
options.B: In a reasonable manner appropriate to the circumstances and in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles
options.C: Equally over all future imports regardless of evidence
options.D: According to the highest possible estimate

questions.explanation: Reasonable documented apportionment is required.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The buyer supplied free molds used to manufacture the imported goods. They are not already included in the invoice. What is the correct customs valuation concept?

options.A: An assist under Article 8.1(b) that may need to be added
options.B: A buying commission
options.C: A prohibited minimum value
options.D: A post-import deduction

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the safest summary formula for transaction value?

options.A: Transaction value equals domestic price plus freight
options.B: Transaction value equals cost of production plus profit in every case
options.C: Transaction value equals the highest available market price
options.D: Transaction value equals price actually paid or payable plus only those Article 8 additions that lawfully apply

questions.explanation: This is the safest summary formula.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What institutions are established under Article 18?

options.A: Only a single national committee in each Member
options.B: The SPS and TBT Committees
options.C: The Committee on Customs Valuation at the WTO and the Technical Committee on Customs Valuation under the WCO/CCC
options.D: Only the Dispute Settlement Body

questions.explanation: Article 18 establishes these institutions.

questions.question: A customs officer discovers buyer and seller are related and immediately rejects transaction value without explanation. Which core rule has been ignored?

options.A: Relationship alone is not enough; customs must examine whether the relationship influenced the price and communicate concerns to the importer
options.B: Article 7 minimum values
options.C: Article 12 publication requirements
options.D: Article 8.2 on freight and insurance

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone does not automatically invalidate Method 1.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 8.1(a)(iii) include?

options.A: Only materials, never labour
options.B: Only labour, never materials
options.C: Only post-import repacking costs
options.D: The cost of packing, whether for labour or materials

questions.explanation: Packing costs include both labour and materials.

tests.title: Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994 - Main Test 12
instructions: Answer all 50 multiple-choice questions.
Difficulty increases gradually across the 12 tests.
Choose the single best answer based on the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of GATT 1994.
Correct answers are distributed to avoid obvious patterns, including avoiding the same correct letter on consecutive questions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Must the valuation methods be applied in sequence?

options.A: No, customs may choose freely
options.B: Yes, except that Articles 5 and 6 may be reversed at the importer’s request
options.C: Only Articles 1 to 3 must be sequential
options.D: Only the importer chooses the method

questions.explanation: The methods follow a sequence, with the Article 4 reversal option.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? If more than one transaction value of similar goods is found under Article 3, which one is used?

options.A: The latest one chronologically
options.B: The one with the most expensive freight
options.C: The highest such value
options.D: The lowest such value

questions.explanation: The lowest qualifying value is selected.

questions.question: What does Article 23 require?

options.A: The Technical Committee must hear appeals annually
options.B: Each Member must recalculate all customs values yearly
options.C: The Committee must review annually the implementation and operation of the Agreement and report developments to the Council for Trade in Goods
options.D: The importer must file annual valuation returns

questions.explanation: Article 23 provides for review.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What reservation may developing countries seek regarding Article 4?

options.A: They may reserve the right to allow reversal of Articles 5 and 6 only when customs authorities agree to the request
options.B: They may reserve the right to avoid all Article 8 additions
options.C: They may reserve the right to skip directly to Article 7
options.D: They may reserve the right to use domestic prices always

questions.explanation: Annex III mentions this possible reservation.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What charges are excluded from customs value if distinguished from the price actually paid or payable under the note to Article 1?

options.A: All commissions and brokerage
options.B: Post-importation construction, erection, assembly, maintenance, or technical assistance, post-import transport, and duties and taxes of the country of importation
options.C: All freight and insurance before importation
options.D: All royalties and licence fees

questions.explanation: Certain post-import and national-tax charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: What does Article 17 preserve for customs administrations?

options.A: The right to ignore Article 1
options.B: The right to avoid appeal procedures
options.C: The right to use arbitrary values
options.D: The right to satisfy themselves as to the truth or accuracy of any statement, document, or declaration presented for customs valuation purposes

questions.explanation: Article 17 preserves customs’ verification powers.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs wants to use computed value, but the foreign producer refuses to provide cost accounts and has no presence in the importing country. What practical limitation is illustrated?

options.A: Computed value is difficult when the producer is unwilling to provide the necessary information and cannot be compelled as a non-resident
options.B: Article 6 requires domestic market prices only
options.C: Article 6 can only be used for identical goods
options.D: Article 6 automatically becomes Article 7

questions.explanation: The Agreement limits compulsion of non-resident producers.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What types of restrictions do not prevent use of transaction value under Article 1.1(a)?

options.A: Only restrictions imposed by carriers
options.B: Only restrictions imposed after importation
options.C: Restrictions imposed by law or public authorities, restrictions limiting geographical area of resale, and restrictions that do not substantially affect the value of the goods
options.D: Any restriction imposed by the seller

questions.explanation: These three categories do not automatically bar Method 1.

questions.question: What are the four main Article 1 conditions for accepting transaction value?

options.A: Identical goods, similar goods, deductive value, and computed value
options.B: No disqualifying restrictions, no undeterminable conditions, no unadjusted proceeds accruing to the seller, and no unacceptable related-party influence on price
options.C: Existence of a bill of lading, packing list, certificate of origin, and customs declaration
options.D: Use of CIF terms, insurance, invoice, and appeal rights

questions.explanation: These are the core Article 1.1 conditions.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What right does Article 16 give the importer?

options.A: The right to choose the exchange rate
options.B: The right to waive Article 8 additions
options.C: The right to stop all customs inquiries
options.D: Upon written request, the importer has the right to a written explanation of how the customs value was determined

questions.explanation: Importers can request a written explanation.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer presents an invoice showing a real sale for export, but the contract says the buyer must also purchase large quantities of unrelated goods, and the value of that condition cannot be determined for the imported goods. What is the main Article 1 problem?

options.A: A currency conversion issue under Article 9
options.B: A publication problem under Article 12
options.C: A condition or consideration under Article 1.1(b) whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued
options.D: A lawful geographical resale restriction

questions.explanation: An undeterminable condition can defeat transaction value.

questions.question: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

options.A: Article 11 gives a right of appeal without penalty
options.B: Article 22 on national legislation
options.C: Article 9 on currency conversion
options.D: Article 13 on release of goods

questions.explanation: Article 11 protects the right of appeal.

questions.question: Scenario variant: A customs officer discovers buyer and seller are related and immediately rejects transaction value without explanation. Which core rule has been ignored?

options.A: Article 12 publication requirements
options.B: Relationship alone is not enough; customs must examine whether the relationship influenced the price and communicate concerns to the importer
options.C: Article 7 minimum values
options.D: Article 8.2 on freight and insurance

questions.explanation: Relatedness alone does not automatically invalidate Method 1.

questions.question: The invoice price excludes post-import inland transport and installation charges, and these are clearly distinguished from the price actually paid or payable. How are they usually treated?

options.A: They must always be added as Article 8 costs
options.B: They require use of minimum values
options.C: They automatically convert the method to Article 5
options.D: They may be excluded from customs value if properly distinguished in line with the note to Article 1

questions.explanation: Certain post-import charges can be excluded if distinguished.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Transaction value cannot be used. Customs finds two possible identical-goods transaction values that both satisfy Article 2. Which should be used?

options.A: Their average
options.B: Whichever value customs prefers
options.C: The lower of the two qualifying values
options.D: The higher of the two qualifying values

questions.explanation: Under Article 2 the lowest qualifying value is used.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? How many main customs valuation methods are set out in Articles 1 through 7?

options.A: Six methods
options.B: Seven methods
options.C: Five methods
options.D: Four methods

questions.explanation: There are six main methods.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Are minimum customs values allowed under Article 7?

options.A: Only for related-party transactions
options.B: No
options.C: Yes, if published in advance
options.D: Only for developing countries permanently

questions.explanation: Minimum customs values are prohibited.

questions.question: The importer pays a royalty for a trademark connected to the imported goods, and payment is required by the seller as a condition of sale. What is the likely treatment?

options.A: The royalty may need to be added if it relates to the goods, is a condition of sale, and is not already included in the price
options.B: It becomes an Article 5 deduction
options.C: It must always be excluded
options.D: It is only relevant after domestic resale

questions.explanation: This fits the core royalty test.

questions.question: Scenario variant: The importer pays a royalty for a trademark connected to the imported goods, and payment is required by the seller as a condition of sale. What is the likely treatment?

options.A: It must always be excluded
options.B: It becomes an Article 5 deduction
options.C: The royalty may need to be added if it relates to the goods, is a condition of sale, and is not already included in the price
options.D: It is only relevant after domestic resale

questions.explanation: This fits the core royalty test.

questions.question: The importer pays a royalty for a trademark connected to the imported goods, and payment is required by the seller as a condition of sale. What is the likely treatment?

options.A: It is only relevant after domestic resale
options.B: It must always be excluded
options.C: It becomes an Article 5 deduction
options.D: The royalty may need to be added if it relates to the goods, is a condition of sale, and is not already included in the price

questions.explanation: This fits the core royalty test.

questions.question: What is one responsibility of the Technical Committee under Annex II?

options.A: To examine specific technical problems in daily administration of the valuation system and give advisory opinions on solutions
options.B: To publish all commercial invoices
options.C: To set national exchange rates
options.D: To hear judicial appeals

questions.explanation: The Technical Committee deals with technical interpretation problems.

questions.question: Imported goods are sold after importation in the local market to unrelated buyers in different quantities and at different prices. Customs needs a deductive value. What concept is most important?

options.A: The importer’s preferred price
options.B: The highest resale price found anywhere
options.C: The producer’s fabrication cost
options.D: The unit price at the greatest aggregate quantity

questions.explanation: Greatest aggregate quantity is central to Article 5.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What are 'goods of the same class or kind' under Article 15?

options.A: Only locally produced goods
options.B: Only the same shipment lot
options.C: Goods falling within a group or range of goods produced by a particular industry or industry sector, including identical or similar goods
options.D: Only goods with the same HS code at any level

questions.explanation: This definition is broader than identical or similar goods.

questions.question: If the importer requests it, how must customs communicate its grounds in a related-party case?

options.A: Only through the seller
options.B: In writing
options.C: Only to the carrier
options.D: Verbally only

questions.explanation: The grounds must be provided in writing on request.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What is the safest summary formula for transaction value?

options.A: Transaction value equals cost of production plus profit in every case
options.B: Transaction value equals domestic price plus freight
options.C: Transaction value equals price actually paid or payable plus only those Article 8 additions that lawfully apply
options.D: Transaction value equals the highest available market price

questions.explanation: This is the safest summary formula.

questions.question: Customs valuation is delayed, but the importer urgently needs the goods for production. What safeguard may be available?

options.A: Immediate judicial appeal only
options.B: Automatic waiver of all duties
options.C: Mandatory use of fallback valuation
options.D: Release of goods against a sufficient guarantee under Article 13

questions.explanation: Article 13 protects the importer when valuation is delayed.

questions.question: What does Article 1.1(c) deal with?

options.A: Only transport costs after importation
options.B: Proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use of the goods that accrue directly or indirectly to the seller
options.C: Only inland warehousing charges
options.D: Only post-clearance penalties

questions.explanation: Article 1.1(c) addresses resale proceeds flowing back to the seller.

questions.question: Imported goods are sold after importation in the local market to unrelated buyers in different quantities and at different prices. Customs needs a deductive value. What concept is most important?

options.A: The unit price at the greatest aggregate quantity
options.B: The highest resale price found anywhere
options.C: The importer’s preferred price
options.D: The producer’s fabrication cost

questions.explanation: Greatest aggregate quantity is central to Article 5.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

options.A: Article 13 on release of goods
options.B: Article 11 gives a right of appeal without penalty
options.C: Article 9 on currency conversion
options.D: Article 22 on national legislation

questions.explanation: Article 11 protects the right of appeal.

questions.question: The buyer supplied free molds used to manufacture the imported goods. They are not already included in the invoice. What is the correct customs valuation concept?

options.A: A buying commission
options.B: A prohibited minimum value
options.C: An assist under Article 8.1(b) that may need to be added
options.D: A post-import deduction

questions.explanation: Buyer-supplied production tools can be assists.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer presents an invoice showing a real sale for export, but the contract says the buyer must also purchase large quantities of unrelated goods, and the value of that condition cannot be determined for the imported goods. What is the main Article 1 problem?

options.A: A publication problem under Article 12
options.B: A currency conversion issue under Article 9
options.C: A lawful geographical resale restriction
options.D: A condition or consideration under Article 1.1(b) whose value cannot be determined with respect to the goods being valued

questions.explanation: An undeterminable condition can defeat transaction value.

questions.question: Customs valuation is delayed, but the importer urgently needs the goods for production. What safeguard may be available?

options.A: Release of goods against a sufficient guarantee under Article 13
options.B: Mandatory use of fallback valuation
options.C: Automatic waiver of all duties
options.D: Immediate judicial appeal only

questions.explanation: Article 13 protects the importer when valuation is delayed.

questions.question: What does Article 1.1(c) deal with?

options.A: Only post-clearance penalties
options.B: Only transport costs after importation
options.C: Proceeds of subsequent resale, disposal, or use of the goods that accrue directly or indirectly to the seller
options.D: Only inland warehousing charges

questions.explanation: Article 1.1(c) addresses resale proceeds flowing back to the seller.

questions.question: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

options.A: Article 11 gives a right of appeal without penalty
options.B: Article 13 on release of goods
options.C: Article 22 on national legislation
options.D: Article 9 on currency conversion

questions.explanation: Article 11 protects the right of appeal.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does the note to Article 7 say about the fallback method?

options.A: Fallback must always use domestic market prices
options.B: Fallback requires minimum customs values
options.C: Fallback may use any arbitrary value
options.D: Fallback valuation should, as far as possible, be based on previously determined customs values and use the methods of Articles 1 to 6 with reasonable flexibility

questions.explanation: The note limits fallback through reasonable flexibility.

questions.question: What are 'similar goods' under Article 15?

options.A: Goods that cannot substitute for one another
options.B: Goods that, although not alike in all respects, have like characteristics and component materials, perform the same functions, and are commercially interchangeable
options.C: Goods produced only in the importing country
options.D: Goods that are identical in appearance only

questions.explanation: Similar goods need not be identical but must be commercially comparable.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does Article 8.1(a)(i) include?

options.A: Commissions and brokerage, except buying commissions
options.B: Only inland taxes
options.C: Only buying commissions
options.D: Only post-import transport

questions.explanation: Selling commissions and brokerage can be additions; buying commissions are excluded.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What does Article 8.2 allow each Member to decide in its legislation?

options.A: Whether to ignore Article 8 completely
options.B: Whether to replace transaction value with minimum values
options.C: Whether to include or exclude in whole or in part transport costs, loading/unloading and handling charges, and insurance costs to the port or place of importation
options.D: Whether to use only deductive value

questions.explanation: Members choose their Article 8.2 treatment in national law.

questions.question: What does the note to Article 8 say a buying commission means?

options.A: A domestic resale commission paid after importation
options.B: A customs broker’s duty calculation fee
options.C: A fee paid to the seller’s agent to secure the sale
options.D: A fee paid by an importer to the importer’s agent for representing the importer abroad in the purchase of the goods being valued

questions.explanation: This is the note’s definition of a buying commission.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? What does the note to Article 1 say about activities undertaken by the buyer on the buyer’s own account?

options.A: They are always added to customs value
options.B: They are not treated as indirect payments to the seller unless Article 8 specifically requires adjustment
options.C: They replace Article 8 additions
options.D: They always bar use of transaction value

questions.explanation: Buyer’s own activities are not automatically dutiable.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? Can substitute values be established under Article 1.2(b)?

options.A: No; the tests are used only for comparison purposes at the initiative of the importer
options.B: Yes; but only for related importers
options.C: Yes; customs may freely create substitute values
options.D: Only when Article 7 is applied first

questions.explanation: Test values are not substitute valuation methods.

questions.question: Does the mere fact that buyer and seller are related automatically make transaction value unacceptable?

options.A: Yes
options.B: Only in sea freight
options.C: Only where transport costs are missing
options.D: No

questions.explanation: Relationship alone is not enough.

questions.question: Scenario variant: An importer challenges a customs valuation determination and worries that simply appealing will itself attract a punishment. Which provision addresses this?

options.A: Article 13 on release of goods
options.B: Article 11 gives a right of appeal without penalty
options.C: Article 22 on national legislation
options.D: Article 9 on currency conversion

questions.explanation: Article 11 protects the right of appeal.

questions.question: In revision, which statement is accurate? What deductions are made under Article 5?

options.A: Only insurance and freight
options.B: Only customs duty and VAT, with no other deductions
options.C: Commissions or usual profit and general expenses, inland transport and insurance, Article 8(2) costs where appropriate, and customs duties and other national taxes
options.D: Only post-import storage

questions.explanation: These are the main deductions under Article 5.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Transaction value cannot be used. Customs finds two possible identical-goods transaction values that both satisfy Article 2. Which should be used?

options.A: Their average
options.B: Whichever value customs prefers
options.C: The higher of the two qualifying values
options.D: The lower of the two qualifying values

questions.explanation: Under Article 2 the lowest qualifying value is used.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? Does the mere fact that buyer and seller are related automatically make transaction value unacceptable?

options.A: No
options.B: Yes
options.C: Only where transport costs are missing
options.D: Only in sea freight

questions.explanation: Relationship alone is not enough.

questions.question: Scenario variant: Customs wants to use computed value, but the foreign producer refuses to provide cost accounts and has no presence in the importing country. What practical limitation is illustrated?

options.A: Article 6 can only be used for identical goods
options.B: Computed value is difficult when the producer is unwilling to provide the necessary information and cannot be compelled as a non-resident
options.C: Article 6 requires domestic market prices only
options.D: Article 6 automatically becomes Article 7

questions.explanation: The Agreement limits compulsion of non-resident producers.

questions.question: A Member faces a recurring technical problem on how to apply the valuation rules to a new business model and wants interpretative guidance. Which body is most relevant?

options.A: Only the importing country’s carrier
options.B: Only the Council for Trade in Goods
options.C: The Technical Committee on Customs Valuation
options.D: Only the producer’s domestic tax authority

questions.explanation: The Technical Committee handles technical interpretation issues.

questions.question: Which option best reflects the Agreement? When are royalties and licence fees added under Article 8.1(c)?

options.A: Whenever any royalty exists in the company
options.B: When they relate to the goods being valued, must be paid directly or indirectly by the buyer as a condition of sale, and are not already included in the price actually paid or payable
options.C: Only where the importer is unrelated to the seller
options.D: Only when paid after domestic resale

questions.explanation: These are the core royalty tests.

questions.question: An officer proposes using the domestic selling price of locally produced goods because it is easy and gives a higher value. What is the correct response?

options.A: It is allowed if customs explains the reason
options.B: It is allowed only for related-party sales
options.C: That basis is prohibited under Article 7
options.D: It is required where Article 1 fails

questions.explanation: Domestic selling price of locally produced goods is prohibited.
